results.possibleIdentity: Kenneth N. Flaxman is an attorney based in Chicago who began his professional life as an electrical engineer. During the late 1960s, he was a student at the City College of New York (CCNY), where he earned a Bachelor of Engineering. Historical records, including amateur radio callbooks from 1965 and 1967, list him as a resident of Tiebout Avenue in the Bronx, New York, with the amateur radio call sign WB2ESZ. His background in electronics and engineering aligns with the profiles maintained by the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) technical service, which used index cards to match students and professional engineers with artists for collaborative projects. After working as an electrical engineer, Flaxman transitioned to the legal profession, receiving a J.D. from the Chicago-Kent College of Law in 1972. He eventually founded the Law Offices of Kenneth N. Flaxman P.C. in Chicago, specializing in civil rights, police misconduct, and class action litigation.
